title: Hieracium subvirenticeps
taxonomy: Hieracium subvirenticeps belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is due to its characteristics and similarities with other plants within the Asteraceae family. Within this family, Hieracium subvirenticeps is further classified as a member of the tribe Cichorieae. The genus Hieracium, to which Hieracium subvirenticeps belongs, is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. This genus is characterized by its annual or perennial herbaceous plants with leaves and flower heads similar to those found in the sunflower family.

intro: Hieracium subvirenticeps is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, commonly known as a hawkweed, which is a perennial herb characterized by its yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subvirenticeps include sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium subvirenticeps is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
